summary: The missing-pair output can contain every one of the T valid subtasks, giving a linear output lower bound; recursive enumeration plus indexed anti-join matches it.

optimality.argument: When Executed is empty, all T legal task-subtask pairs must be emitted, so any exact query requires Omega(T) output work. The recursive CTE generates every legal pair once and performs one composite-key anti-join probe per pair, for O(T) total time.

replacement_checks.evidence: An empty Executed table forces the result to contain all T possible task-subtask pairs.
Each recursive row increments one task-local subtask identifier, producing exactly T rows, and each row receives one indexed NOT EXISTS probe.
Authored fixtures cover mixed completion, empty executions, full execution, noncontiguous gaps, independent task ranges, unsorted source rows, and a missing final subtask.
